---
title: "Client Report - Unit 1 Task 1: Exploring Names"
subtitle: "Course DS 250"
author: "Vincent Gentry"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    page-layout: full
    title-block-banner: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: body
    number-sections: false
    html-math-method: katex
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show the code"
    code-overflow: wrap
    code-copy: hover
    code-tools:
      source: false
      toggle: true
      caption: See code
execute:
  warning: false

---

```{python}
import polars as pl
from lets_plot import *

LetsPlot.setup_html()

# Primary yearly dataset used for most questions
url_year = "https://github.com/byuidatascience/data4names/raw/master/data-raw/names_year/names_year.csv"
# Probability dataset used to estimate female usage by name
url_prob = "https://github.com/byuidatascience/data4names/raw/master/data-raw/names_prob/names_prob.csv"

names_year = pl.read_csv(url_year, ignore_errors=True)
names_prob = pl.read_csv(url_prob, ignore_errors=True)

names_year.head()
```

## Client Request: Name Trends Over Time

__How has usage changed over time for Mary, Martha, Peter, Paul, and a famous movie name (Elsa)?__

```{python}
focus_names = ["Mary", "Martha", "Peter", "Paul", "Elsa"]

focus_trends = (
    names_year
    .filter(pl.col("name").is_in(focus_names))
    .select(["name", "year", "Total"])
    .sort(["name", "year"])
)

focus_trends.head(10)
```

```{python}
(
    ggplot(focus_trends, aes(x="year", y="Total", color="name"))
    + geom_line(size=1)
    + labs(
        title="Usage Over Time for Selected Names",
        x="Year",
        y="Number of Babies"
    )
)
```

_The trend chart shows that long-established names like Mary, Martha, Peter, and Paul appear across many decades, while Elsa shows a more modern pattern._

## QUESTION|TASK 1

__What was the earliest year that the name 'Felisha' was used?__

```{python}
felisha = names_year.filter(pl.col("name") == "Felisha")
earliest_year = felisha.select(pl.col("year").min()).item()

print(f"Earliest year 'Felisha' was used: {earliest_year}")
felisha.sort("year").head(5)
```

_The earliest year that the name Felisha appeared in this dataset was **`{python} earliest_year`**._

## QUESTION|TASK 2

__What year had the most babies named 'David'? How many babies were named 'David' that year?__

```{python}
david_by_year = (
    names_year
    .filter(pl.col("name") == "David")
    .group_by("year")
    .agg(pl.col("Total").sum().alias("total_count"))
    .sort("total_count", descending=True)
)

peak_year_david = david_by_year.row(0)[0]
peak_count_david = david_by_year.row(0)[1]

print(f"Peak year for David: {peak_year_david} with {peak_count_david:,} babies")
david_by_year.head(5)
```

```{python}
(
    ggplot(david_by_year.sort("year"), aes(x="year", y="total_count"))
    + geom_line(color="#1f77b4", size=1)
    + geom_vline(xintercept=peak_year_david, linetype="dashed", color="red")
    + labs(
        title="Babies Named David by Year",
        x="Year",
        y="Number of Babies"
    )
)
```

_The year with the highest number of babies named David was **`{python} peak_year_david`**, with **`{python} f"{peak_count_david:,}"`** babies._

## QUESTION|TASK 3

__What year did your name hit its peak? How many babies were named your name in that year?__

```{python}
my_name = "Vincent"

my_name_by_year = (
    names_year
    .filter(pl.col("name") == my_name)
    .group_by("year")
    .agg(pl.col("Total").sum().alias("total_count"))
    .sort("total_count", descending=True)
)

my_peak_year = my_name_by_year.row(0)[0]
my_peak_count = my_name_by_year.row(0)[1]

print(f"Peak year for {my_name}: {my_peak_year} with {my_peak_count:,} babies")
my_name_by_year.head(5)
```

```{python}
(
    ggplot(my_name_by_year.sort("year"), aes(x="year", y="total_count"))
    + geom_line(color="#2ca02c", size=1)
    + geom_vline(xintercept=my_peak_year, linetype="dashed", color="red")
    + labs(
        title=f"Babies Named {my_name} by Year",
        x="Year",
        y="Number of Babies"
    )
)
```

_The peak year for my name, Vincent, was **`{python} my_peak_year`**, with **`{python} f"{my_peak_count:,}"`** babies._

## QUESTION|TASK 4

__In the most recent year of data, what was the most common female name in Utah?__

```{python}
# names_year is a wide table with no sex-by-year column.
# Estimate female Utah counts using prob_female from names_prob.
most_recent_year = names_year.select(pl.col("year").max()).item()

utah_female_est = (
    names_year
    .filter((pl.col("year") == most_recent_year) & pl.col("UT").is_not_null())
    .select(["name", "UT"])
    .join(
        names_prob.select(["name", "prob_female"]),
        on="name",
        how="left"
    )
    .with_columns(
        (pl.col("UT") * pl.col("prob_female").fill_null(0.0)).alias("estimated_female_ut")
    )
    .sort("estimated_female_ut", descending=True)
    .select(["name", "estimated_female_ut"])
    .head(10)
)

top_female_name_ut = utah_female_est.row(0)[0]
top_female_count_ut = utah_female_est.row(0)[1]

print(f"Most recent year in data: {most_recent_year}")
print(f"Top female name in Utah (estimated): {top_female_name_ut} ({top_female_count_ut:,.0f})")
utah_female_est
```

_In **`{python} most_recent_year`**, the most common female name in Utah was estimated to be **`{python} top_female_name_ut`**, with about **`{python} f"{top_female_count_ut:,.0f}"`** babies._
